title: Why a Tesla and SpaceX Merger Would Not Be Great News for Tesla Shareholders
summary: A potential merger between Tesla and SpaceX, though speculated due to their shared CEO Elon Musk, is unlikely to benefit Tesla shareholders. While some collaboration exists, a merger goes against the current trend of companies splitting into focused entities, could dilute Tesla's stock value before its anticipated revenue ramp-up from robotaxis and Optimus robots, and would burden Tesla shareholders with SpaceX's significant capital investments.

title: Will axing this 174-year-old brand boost Lloyds’ share price?
summary: Lloyds' share price has performed strongly, and the bank is reportedly considering shuttering its 174-year-old Halifax brand. This move could strengthen Lloyds' market presence, cut costs by reducing branch numbers and product duplication, and help it compete better against challenger banks. However, the author remains cautious about buying Lloyds shares due to potential minimal impact from brand streamlining and broader economic risks like sinking loan demand and rising impairments.

title: TSLA Stock Slips Overnight: SpaceX IPO Hype Is Starting To Look Like Amazon's Dot-Com Bubble Peak In 2000, Warns Ex-Lehman Trader
summary: A former Lehman Brothers trader, Larry McDonald, warns that the current hype around SpaceX's potential IPO, alongside OpenAI and Anthropic, mirrors the dot-com bubble's peak in 2000, specifically referencing Amazon's subsequent 90% stock collapse. He expresses concerns about the "astronomically unrealistic" valuations and the risk posed to passive investors and retirement funds due to potential rapid index inclusion, likening the current market environment to being "drunk on a narrative" driven by AI. Tesla's stock (TSLA) slipped overnight amidst this speculation, though it had recorded its best month since September.

summary: Michael Burry, known for "The Big Short," has taken new short positions against several major companies, including Caterpillar Inc., Nvidia Corp., Applied Materials Inc., and Tesla Inc., along with the iShares Semiconductor ETF. Burry's primary concern with Caterpillar stems from what he perceives as overvaluation, particularly as the company has been seen as an AI infrastructure beneficiary, leading its stock to reach record highs with an 86% gain in the first half of 2026. He believes the stock's price-to-sales ratio has reached an unsustainable level, indicating the "beginning of the end" for its rally.

title: Tesla Q2 deliveries expected to rebound as Europe sales recover
summary: Tesla is projected to report a 5% increase in vehicle deliveries for the second quarter, driven by a significant rebound in European demand due to rising fuel prices. Analysts anticipate deliveries to reach 402,780 vehicles, with Europe showing nearly 40% growth.

title: General Mills stock rises 4% on earnings beat despite weak guidance
summary: General Mills (NYSE:GIS) reported fourth-quarter results exceeding analyst expectations, with adjusted EPS of $0.95 and revenue of $4.6 billion. Despite this earnings beat, the company's fiscal 2027 outlook for adjusted EPS ($3.00-$3.20) fell below consensus estimates of $3.41, leading shares to rise 4.05% in pre-market trading. General Mills plans to generate $3 billion in cumulative cost savings by fiscal 2030, with a significant portion expected in fiscal 2027, driven by productivity programs and global transformation initiatives.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Flags 'Extreme Shortage' While DRAM ETF Smashes $25 Billion, Surging Over 170% Since L
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook has highlighted an "extreme shortage" in the hardware sector, driven by unprecedented demand fueled by the AI revolution. Meanwhile, the DRAM ETF (BATS: DRAM) has seen a historic rise since its launch on April 2, 2026, amassing over $25 billion and surging more than 170%, primarily benefiting memory and semiconductor companies due to this supply crunch. This disparity is causing increased input costs for tech giants like Apple and Tesla, while memory chip makers experience significant profits.

title: Tesla's Cybercab is now testing without a steering wheel in Austin
summary: Tesla is advancing the testing of its Cybercab robotaxi in Austin, with a recent video showing a version operating without a steering wheel or pedals. This development follows months of Cybercabs being seen in Austin, regulatory steps like the upload of a first responder interaction plan, and proposed NHTSA revisions for autonomous vehicles. Despite these advancements, Tesla's existing robotaxi fleet has faced crashes and public skepticism, while the company also deals with lawsuits related to its automated driving systems.

title: New Tesla sales in Spain rise 5.6% year-on-year in June
summary: Tesla's new car sales in Spain increased by 5.6% year-on-year in June, totaling 2,779 vehicles. For the first six months of the year, Tesla sales in Spain rose by 29.8% compared to the same period in 2025. This growth outpaced the overall 37.9% increase in electrified vehicle sales, which includes fully-electric and hybrid models.
